translations.idiomatic: He nudged Wang out of the county with polite escorts instead of an arrest.

sentences.zh: 林卿素驕，慚於賓客，並度其為變，儲兵馬以待之。

translations.literal: Linqing was by nature arrogant and ashamed before his retainers; Bing guessed he would turn violent and stored horses and weapons to await him.
translations.idiomatic: He guessed Wang's pride would spark violence and readied troops.

sentences.zh: 林卿既去，北度涇橋，令騎奴還至寺門，拔刀剝其建鼓。

translations.literal: After Linqing departed, crossing north over the Jing Bridge, he had a mounted slave ride back to the county temple gate, draw a knife, and strip the drum's hide.
translations.idiomatic: Wang's slave peeled the county drum at the gate as a taunt.

sentences.zh: 併自從吏兵追林卿。

translations.literal: Bing personally led clerks and soldiers in pursuit of Linqing.
translations.idiomatic: He Bing led the chase himself.

sentences.zh: 行數十里，林卿迫窘，及令奴冠其冠被其襜褕自代，乘車從童騎，身變服從間徑馳去。

translations.literal: After several dozen li Linqing was pressed tight; he ordered a slave to wear his cap and outer robe as a substitute, ride the carriage with boy attendants, while he changed clothes and galloped off by a side path.
translations.idiomatic: Wang dressed a slave as himself and slipped away in disguise.

sentences.zh: 會日暮追及，收縛冠奴，奴曰：「我非侍中，奴耳。」

translations.literal: At dusk they caught up, seized and bound the capped slave; the slave said, "I am not the gentleman attendant—I am only a slave."
translations.idiomatic: At dusk they caught the decoy, who shouted that he was only a slave.

sentences.zh: 併自知已失林卿，乃曰：「王君困，自稱奴，得脫死邪？」

translations.literal: Bing knew he had already lost Linqing and said, "Lord Wang, pressed tight, calls himself a slave—can that escape death?"
translations.idiomatic: He Bing sneered that a gentleman calling himself a slave still owed the law a head.

sentences.zh: 叱吏斷頭持還，縣所剝鼓置都亭下，署曰；

translations.literal: He ordered clerks to cut off the head and bring it back, hung the stripped drum where the drum had been at the relay station, and posted a placard reading;
translations.idiomatic: He took the slave's head and nailed a notice beside the ruined drum—

sentences.zh: 「故侍中王林卿坐殺人埋塚捨，使奴剝寺門鼓。」

translations.literal: "Former gentleman attendant Wang Linqing sat guilty of killing a man and burying him in the tomb keeper's hut, and ordered a slave to strip the temple gate drum."
translations.idiomatic: —accusing Wang Linqing of murder and drum-stripping.

sentences.zh: 吏民驚駭。

translations.literal: Clerks and people were stunned.
translations.idiomatic: The county gaped at the spectacle.

sentences.zh: 林卿因亡命，眾庶言雚嘩，以為實死。

translations.literal: Linqing therefore fled his life; the crowd clamored, thinking he was truly dead.
translations.idiomatic: Wang fled; rumor mistook the slave's head for his own.

sentences.zh: 成帝太后以邛成太后愛林卿故，聞之涕泣，為言哀帝。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng's empress dowager, because Empress Dowager of Qiongcheng loved Linqing, wept when she heard and spoke to Emperor Ai.
translations.idiomatic: Chengdi's mother wept to Ai for Wang Linqing.

sentences.zh: 哀帝問狀而善之，遷併攏西太守。

translations.literal: Emperor Ai asked the facts, approved them, and promoted Bing to governor of Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Ai approved the sting and moved He Bing to Longxi.

sentences.zh: 徙穎川太守，代陵陽嚴詡。

translations.literal: He was transferred to governor of Yingchuan, replacing Yan Xu of Lingsyang.
translations.idiomatic: He took Yingchuan from the mild magistrate Yan Xu.

sentences.zh: 詡本以孝行為官，謂掾史為師友，有過輒閉閣自責，終不大言。

translations.literal: Xu had originally entered office on filial conduct, treated his clerks as teachers and friends, and when there was fault would shut his doors and blame himself, never speaking loudly.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xu ruled by shame and silence.

sentences.zh: 郡中亂，王莽遣使徵詡，官屬數百人為設祖道，詡據地哭。

translations.literal: The commandery was in disorder; Wang Mang sent an agent to summon Xu; several hundred subordinates set out a farewell road for him, and Xu sat on the ground weeping.
translations.idiomatic: When Mang recalled Yan Xu, hundreds of clerks wept at his leaving.

sentences.zh: 掾史曰：「明府吉徵，不宜若此。」

translations.literal: The clerks said, "The governor has a lucky summons—you should not be like this."
translations.idiomatic: They said a promotion merited joy, not tears.

sentences.zh: 詡曰：「吾哀穎川士，身豈有憂哉！

translations.literal: Xu said, "I grieve for the gentlemen of Yingchuan—how should I have worry for myself!
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xu said he wept for the people, not himself—

sentences.zh: 我以柔弱徵，必選剛猛代。

translations.literal: I am summoned for weakness; they will surely choose someone harsh to replace me.
translations.idiomatic: —because a soft governor meant a hard replacement.

sentences.zh: 代到，將有僵仆者，故相吊耳。」

translations.literal: When the replacement arrives there will be men who fall stiff—therefore I mourn in advance."
translations.idiomatic: Men would die when the new man arrived, he said."

sentences.zh: 詡至，拜為美俗使者。

translations.literal: When Xu arrived he was appointed commissioner for fine customs.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Xu was made a roving "fine customs" commissioner.

sentences.zh: 是時，穎川鐘元為尚書令，領廷尉，用事有權。

translations.literal: At that time Zhong Yuan of Yingchuan was master of writing and concurrent commandant of justice, wielding power.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Yuan ran the secretariat and the courts together.

sentences.zh: 弟威為郡掾，臧千金。

translations.literal: His younger brother Wei was a commandery clerk and had embezzled a thousand in gold.
translations.idiomatic: Brother Zhong Wei had skimmed a thousand gold as a clerk.

sentences.zh: 併為太守，過辭鐘廷尉，廷尉免冠為弟請一等之罪，願蚤就髡鉗。

translations.literal: When Bing became governor he paid a farewell visit to Commandant Zhong; the commandant removed his cap and begged for his younger brother's crime to be reduced one degree, wishing him soon to accept shaved head and iron collar.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Yuan begged bareheaded for his brother.

sentences.zh: 併曰：「罪在弟身與君律，不在於太守。」

translations.literal: Bing said, "The crime is on the younger brother's person and your lordship's statutes—it does not lie with the governor."
translations.idiomatic: He Bing said the law bound the brother, not the governor's mercy.

sentences.zh: 元懼，馳遣人呼弟。

translations.literal: Yuan was afraid and galloped a messenger to summon his brother.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Yuan panicked and sent for Wei.

sentences.zh: 陽翟輕俠趙季、李款多畜賓客，以氣力漁食閭里，至奸人婦女，持吏長短，從橫郡中，聞並且至，皆亡去。

translations.literal: The Yangdi bravos Zhao Ji and Li Kuan kept many retainers, fed on the hamlets by force of brawn, went so far as to violate others' wives and daughters, held clerks' secrets, and ran wild in the commandery; hearing Bing was about to arrive, they all fled.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Ji and Li Kuan fled when they heard He Bing was coming.

sentences.zh: 併下車求勇猛曉文法吏且十人，使文吏治三人獄，武吏往捕之，各有所部。

translations.literal: Bing on dismounting sought roughly ten fierce clerks who understood statutes, had civil clerks build three case files, and sent military clerks to arrest them, each with his assignment.
translations.idiomatic: He drafted three dockets and sent civil and military clerks after each target.

sentences.zh: 敕曰：「三人非負太守，乃負王法，不得不治。

translations.literal: He charged them: "The three men do not offend the governor—they offend the king's law and cannot go untried.
translations.idiomatic: His order said the law, not the governor, demanded blood.

sentences.zh: 鐘威所犯多在赦前，驅使入函谷關，勿令污民間；

translations.literal: Most of Zhong Wei's offenses fall before the last amnesty—drive him into the Hangu Pass and do not let him foul the folk;
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Wei should be pushed through Hangu Pass if most counts predated the amnesty—

sentences.zh: 不入關，乃收之。

translations.literal: if he does not enter the pass, then seize him.
translations.idiomatic: —otherwise arrest him locally.

sentences.zh: 趙、李桀惡，雖遠去，當得其頭，以謝百姓。」

translations.literal: Zhao and Li are chief villains; though they flee far, take their heads to answer the hundred surnames."
translations.idiomatic: Bring back Zhao and Li's heads for the market, he said."

sentences.zh: 鐘威負其兄，止雒陽，吏格殺之。

translations.literal: Zhong Wei relied on his elder brother, stopped at Luoyang, and clerks killed him in resistance.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong Wei stalled at Luoyang and died under the clerk's blade.

sentences.zh: 亦得趙、李它郡，持頭還，併皆懸頭及其具獄於市。

translations.literal: Zhao and Li were also taken in other commanderies; heads were brought back, and Bing hung the heads and full case documents in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao and Li died abroad; He Bing spiked their heads with the indictments.

sentences.zh: 郡中清靜，表善好士，見紀穎川，名次黃霸。

translations.literal: The commandery grew quiet; he commended the good and esteemed gentlemen and was recorded in Yingchuan, his name ranking after Huang Ba.
translations.idiomatic: Yingchuan ranked him beside Huang Ba.

sentences.zh: 性清廉，妻子不至官舍。

translations.literal: By nature he was clean and incorrupt; wife and children did not come to the official residence.
translations.idiomatic: His family never moved into the yamen.

sentences.zh: 數年，卒。

translations.literal: After several years he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office a few years later.

sentences.zh: 疾病，召丞掾作先令書，曰：「告子恢，吾生素餐日久，死雖當得法賻，勿受。

translations.literal: When ill he summoned assistant and chief clerk to draft his testament, saying, "Tell my son Hui: I have long eaten free rice; though in death I should receive the statutory burial grant, do not accept it.
translations.idiomatic: He told his son to refuse the state burial grant.

sentences.zh: 葬為小槨，但容下棺。」

translations.literal: Bury me in a small outer coffin that only fits the inner coffin."
translations.idiomatic: He ordered a plain box just wide enough for the inner shell."

sentences.zh: 恢如父言。

translations.literal: Hui followed his father's words.
translations.idiomatic: His son obeyed.

sentences.zh: 王莽擢恢為關都尉。

translations.literal: Wang Mang promoted Hui to colonel of the passes.
translations.idiomatic: Mang made the son a frontier colonel.

sentences.zh: 建武中以併孫為郎。

translations.literal: In the Jianwu era a grandson of Bing was appointed gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Later Guangwudi honored a grandson with a gentleman appointment.
